channel.title: Latest from Android Central
channel.description: All the latest content from the Android Central team

item.title: Googlebook is coming... but what about Chromebooks? Here's what Google has to say
item.description: Questions remain about the future of ChromeOS following the Googlebook announcement.
item.author: sanujb6@gmail.com (Sanuj Bhatia)
item.dc:creator: Sanuj Bhatia

media:content.media:description: Design Elements of Googlebook
media:content.media:text: Design Elements of Googlebook
media:content.media:title: Design Elements of Googlebook

item.title: Your Android security and privacy got huge upgrades—The Android Show reveals all
item.description: The Android Show showcased the many privacy and security upgrades users can expect this year from Android 17.
item.author: nickodiaz@sbcglobal.net (Nickolas Diaz)
item.dc:creator: Nickolas Diaz

media:content.media:description: Android's security was highlighted in length during the Android Show 2026.
media:content.media:text: Android's security was highlighted in length during the Android Show 2026.
media:content.media:title: Android's security was highlighted in length during the Android Show 2026.

item.title: Google wants Gemini to take over how you browse in Chrome
item.description: Google is turning Chrome on Android into a Gemini-powered AI assistant that can summarize pages, edit images, and handle tasks for you.
item.author: techkritiko@gmail.com (Jay Bonggolto)
item.dc:creator: Jay Bonggolto

media:content.media:description: Google Chrome logo on an Android phone held in one hand.
media:content.media:text: Google Chrome logo on an Android phone held in one hand.
media:content.media:title: Google Chrome logo on an Android phone held in one hand.

item.title: I didn't think the desktop cursor needed reinventing — Googlebooks are proving me wrong
item.description: Is Android's desktop mode just going to feel like a blown-up version of Android on your phone? No, and Magic Pointer is one of the reasons why.
item.author: bradypsnyder@gmail.com (Brady Snyder)
item.dc:creator: Brady Snyder

media:content.media:description: Design Elements of Googlebook
media:content.media:text: Design Elements of Googlebook
media:content.media:title: Design Elements of Googlebook

item.title: The iOS-Android file sharing nightmare is officially over for more Android users
item.description: Google is finally making Android sharing and iPhone switching less painful.
item.author: techkritiko@gmail.com (Jay Bonggolto)
item.dc:creator: Jay Bonggolto

media:content.media:description: Quick Settings on the Pixel 10
media:content.media:text: Quick Settings on the Pixel 10
media:content.media:title: Quick Settings on the Pixel 10

item.title: Android Auto gets a fresh take on Google Maps navigation, teases more Gemini at the Android Show
item.description: The Android Show 2026 revealed Google's major personal and AI-fueled plans for Android Auto, giving drivers a refined hands-free experience.
item.author: nickodiaz@sbcglobal.net (Nickolas Diaz)
item.dc:creator: Nickolas Diaz

media:content.media:description: Google maps receives a visual update for Android Auto, introducing "Immersive Navigation" for a more robust 3D view of the world around your car.
media:content.media:text: Google maps receives a visual update for Android Auto, introducing "Immersive Navigation" for a more robust 3D view of the world around your car.
media:content.media:title: Google maps receives a visual update for Android Auto, introducing "Immersive Navigation" for a more robust 3D view of the world around your car.

item.title: Android 17 is fixing two things that have annoyed me for years
item.description: Android 17 introduces Pause Point and new 3D emoji to make phones feel more human.
item.author: sanujb6@gmail.com (Sanuj Bhatia)
item.dc:creator: Sanuj Bhatia

media:content.media:description: Old Android emojis on a Google Pixel phone
media:content.media:text: Old Android emojis on a Google Pixel phone
media:content.media:title: Old Android emojis on a Google Pixel phone

item.title: Not just an OS: Gemini Intelligence shines with Android automation this summer
item.description: Gemini Intelligence and Google's agentic AI plans took center stage during the Android Show 2026, and there are huge features on the way this summer.
item.author: nickodiaz@sbcglobal.net (Nickolas Diaz)
item.dc:creator: Nickolas Diaz

media:content.media:description: The Gemini Era graphic from Google.
media:content.media:text: The Gemini Era graphic from Google.

item.title: Google just revealed what comes after Chromebooks — and it's called Googlebook
item.description: Googlebook is Google's new AI-first laptop category built around Gemini Intelligence.
item.author: sanujb6@gmail.com (Sanuj Bhatia)
item.dc:creator: Sanuj Bhatia

media:content.media:description: Googlebook's main highlight
media:content.media:text: Googlebook's main highlight

item.title: Google is finally treating Android creators seriously with Android 17
item.description: Android 17 adds creator-focused features like AI editing tools, Screen Reactions, better Instagram uploads, and Adobe Premiere support
item.author: sanujb6@gmail.com (Sanuj Bhatia)
item.dc:creator: Sanuj Bhatia

media:content.media:description: The Android 17 logo on the Google Pixel 10.
media:content.media:text: The Android 17 logo on the Google Pixel 10.

item.title: Google will already give you a huge discount on the Fitbit Air — just follow these steps when you preorder
item.description: You can already score a FREE Fitbit Air by trading in an old device at the Google Store

item.title: One UI 9 beta arrives to give Galaxy S26 owners a taste of Android 17
item.description: Samsung’s One UI 9 beta brings Android 17 features, smarter security, and better customization, and it's kicking off this week.

item.title: Samsung Messages is going away. Transferring your conversations to Google Messages is super easy — Here's how

item.title: Motorola Razr Plus 2026 vs. Razr Plus 2025: Differences you can't see
item.description: The Motorola Razr Plus 2026 is an exciting new entry into Motorola's foldable portfolio, but is it really better than the 2025 model? We break it down for you.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Sutrich

media:content.media:description: Motorola Razr Plus 2026 cover screen
media:content.media:text: Motorola Razr Plus 2026 cover screen

item.title: I test audio products for a living, and between the Sony's WH-1000XM6 and Bose QC Ultra Headphones (2nd Gen), this is what I think you should buy
item.description: It's easy to compare ANC performance, but there's so much more to consider when looking at the Sony WH-1000XM6 vs the Bose QC Ultra (2nd Gen).
item.author: klpmediallc@gmail.com (Tshaka Armstrong)
item.dc:creator: Tshaka Armstrong

media:content.media:description: The Bose QC Ultra headphones (2nd Gen) on the left and on the right, the Sony WH-1000XM6

item.title: The 'fantastic' Nothing Phone (3) is one of the most eye-catching devices on the market — and it just scored a major discount at Best Buy

item.title: Samsung's solution for apps with 'excessive' ads is rolling out now

item.title: Yes, it's real — new Verizon deal gives your family four free phones AND unlimited wireless for only $25 per line

item.title: Whoop is putting a board-certified physician in its app to tell you why you’re tired

item.title: The Tom's Guide Savings Squad is packed with experts, opening my eyes to big deals I've never seen

item.title: A buggy Fitbit update is blinding sensors on the Pixel Watch 3 and 4
item.description: Google’s latest Fitbit update is breaking SpO2 and skin temperature tracking on Pixel Watches, leaving users frustrated.

item.dc:creator: Jay Bonggolto

media:content.media:description: A photo of the Google Pixel Watch 4 on a light wooden desk showing the "Steps" Tile, showing a current count of 33,961 with "23k over" and a Daily Goal of 10,000 underneath.

item.title: Ready for a change? Switch from AT&T or Verizon and T-Mobile will give you up to $800 to pay off your phone

item.title: Viture already has the best smart glasses ecosystem, and these two new products somehow make it even better
item.description: I've loved Viture's extended smart glasses ecosystem for years, and now things just got even better with a pair of new releases.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Sutrich

media:content.media:description: Viture Beast smart glasses connected to an Abxylute S9V controller, with Monster Hunter Outlanders playing on a Google Pixel 10 Pro XL
